source.title: Peer Comparison: PDD vs Alibaba vs JD.com — Take Rate, Margins, Business Model Quality

extractions.claim: PDD has the highest operating margin among major Chinese e-commerce platforms at 22-27%, compared to Alibaba's 11-16% and JD's 0-5%
extractions.evidence: PDD operating margin: FY2024 27.5%, 9M 2025 22.1% (compressed by merchant subsidies). Alibaba operating margin: FY2025 (Mar) ~15.6%, Sep 2025 quarter ~11.3% (pressured by quick commerce investment). JD.com operating margin: FY2025 consolidated near breakeven (~0-1% due to food delivery losses), JD Retail segment 4.6%.

verification.verifier_notes: PDD margins from earnings releases. Alibaba margins from MacroTrends and earnings. JD margins from Q3 2025 earnings release. All three companies are currently investing heavily in new initiatives, compressing margins.

extractions.claim: PDD's gross margin (~57%) far exceeds JD (~15%) and is higher than Alibaba (~40%), reflecting pure marketplace model
extractions.evidence: PDD 9M 2025 gross margin: 56.6%. Alibaba gross margin: ~39-41% (includes cloud, logistics). JD.com gross margin: ~15.3% (includes 1P retail with product COGS). PDD's high gross margin reflects its pure marketplace model — no inventory ownership, no logistics network.

verification.verifier_notes: All from latest quarterly earnings. JD's low margin reflects 1P retail model. Alibaba's mid-range reflects diversified business mix.

extractions.claim: Take rate comparison: PDD domestic ~4-5%, Alibaba Taobao/Tmall ~5%, JD ~8-10%, Douyin ~5-10%
extractions.evidence: PDD domestic (Pinduoduo): Bernstein est. 4.2% (FY2023), trending toward 5%. Alibaba: ~5% take rate with improving CMR from Quanzhantui. JD.com: 8-10% including logistics fees. Douyin: 5-10% including livestream service fees. PDD's take rate is lowest but growing; Temu's effective take rate is structurally different (35-40% fully managed).

verification.verifier_notes: Take rates are analyst estimates since none of these companies disclose official take rates. PDD and Alibaba are closest; JD charges more because logistics is bundled.

extractions.claim: PDD's net income margin (~27%) is the highest in Chinese e-commerce, boosted by interest income from RMB 424B cash pile
extractions.evidence: PDD 9M 2025 net margin: ~24.3% (RMB 74.8B net income / RMB 307.9B revenue). FY2024 net margin: 28.5%. Alibaba net margin: ~9-11%. JD.com net margin: ~3-4%. PDD's net income consistently exceeds operating profit because interest/investment income (RMB 19.2B in 9M 2025) more than offsets taxes.

verification.verifier_notes: Net income from earnings releases. The interest income boost is a distinctive PDD feature among Chinese e-commerce peers.
